goal: Land the `notify` + `notify-debouncer-full` based file watcher in `ucil-daemon` so P1-W3-F02 flips to passes=true. Two detection paths: editor/filesystem events arrive through `notify-debouncer-full` with a 100 ms debounce window; PostToolUse hook invocations bypass the debouncer entirely and emit an event immediately. This WO is the foundation for P1-W3-F03 (Watchman auto-detection — separate WO) and for every downstream consumer that turns file events into parse/index work (P1-W4-F04 pipeline).
plan_summary: Introduce `crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs` with typed `FileEvent`/`FileEventKind`/`EventSource`/`WatcherError` + a `FileWatcher` orchestrator that owns a `notify-debouncer-full` instance and forwards debounced filesystem events over a `tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<FileEvent>`. The debouncer runs on its own OS thread (as `notify` does by design); we bridge its `std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<DebounceEventResult>` into async-land via a `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` (or equivalent) forwarder task. A second, synchronous public method `FileWatcher::notify_hook_event(path, kind)` sends the event through the same async channel with `EventSource::PostToolUseHook` and `WITHOUT` going through the debouncer — this models the §14 lines 1024-1025 fast path. A named const `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW: Duration = Duration::from_millis(100)` fixes the editor-event debounce per master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741. All new tests live at module root (flat, no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005; use real `tempfile::TempDir` + real file writes (no mocks of `notify`). Workspace root `Cargo.toml` gains `notify = "6"` and `notify-debouncer-full = "0.3"` under `[workspace.dependencies]`; `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` pulls both via `{ workspace = true }`. No other crate edited. No change to forbidden paths.
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `notify = "6"` and `notify-debouncer-full = "0.3"` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (pinned major; no default-feature disabling — the defaults for notify 6 are already lean). Place entries alphabetically near the existing filesystem deps (e.g., near `tempfile`/`walkdir`) with a short block comment citing master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741 and P1-W3-F02.
crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add `notify = { workspace = true }` and `notify-debouncer-full = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]` (after the existing `toml.workspace = true` line).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod watcher;` declaration and a `pub use watcher::{EventSource, FileEvent, FileEventKind, FileWatcher, WatcherError, DEBOUNCE_WINDOW};` re-export block (keep the existing re-export blocks alphabetical — clippy-pedantic `missing_docs_in_private_items` and `module_name_repetitions` are already tuned in). Extend the module-level rustdoc paragraph block to describe the new `watcher` module in one sentence, citing master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741 + §14 lines 1024-1025 for the two detection paths.
crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — new module. Top of file: `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` ONLY if clippy-pedantic trips on `FileEvent`/`FileWatcher`; prefer fixing first.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub const DEBOUNCE_WINDOW: std::time::Duration = std::time::Duration::from_millis(100);` — named constant per master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741 and §14 line 1025. Rustdoc cites both sections.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub enum FileEventKind { Created, Modified, Removed, Renamed, Other }` deriving `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. `Renamed` covers notify's `Rename` / `Modify::Name` variants. `Other` catches notify kinds that don't map cleanly (metadata-only changes, ACL tweaks, etc.) so the match is total without swallowing unknown events silently.
crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub enum EventSource { NotifyDebounced, PostToolUseHook }` deriving `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. Rustdoc on both variants cites §14 lines 1024-1025.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub struct FileEvent { pub path: std::path::PathBuf, pub kind: FileEventKind, pub source: EventSource }` deriving `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`. No timestamp field in this WO — the event order through the mpsc channel is the only ordering contract we commit to here (timestamps are a §12.1 hot-observation concern, out of scope for F02).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub enum WatcherError` deriving `thiserror::Error, Debug` with `#[non_exhaustive]`, variants: `#[error("notify error: {0}")] Notify(#[from] notify::Error)`, `#[error("debouncer error: {0}")] Debouncer(String)` (since `notify-debouncer-full` surfaces errors as a vector of `notify::Error`, we squash to `String` at the boundary to keep the enum simple), `#[error("watcher channel closed")] ChannelClosed`, `#[error("io error: {0}")] Io(#[from] std::io::Error)`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `pub struct FileWatcher { sender: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<FileEvent>, debouncer: notify_debouncer_full::Debouncer<notify::RecommendedWatcher, notify_debouncer_full::NoCache>, _forwarder: tokio::task::JoinHandle<()> }`. Fields are `pub(crate)` / private; only the constructor + public methods are exposed.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `impl FileWatcher { pub fn new(root: &std::path::Path, sender: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<FileEvent>) -> Result<Self, WatcherError> }`. Spawns the debouncer via `notify_debouncer_full::new_debouncer(DEBOUNCE_WINDOW, None, move |result| { /* push raw result into a std mpsc */ })`, then `tokio::spawn_blocking` a forwarder task that drains the std mpsc and pushes mapped `FileEvent`s into the async sender. Calls `.watcher().watch(root, notify::RecursiveMode::Recursive)?`. `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(sender), fields(root = %root.display()))]` (span name `ucil.daemon.watcher.new` per master-plan §15.2).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `impl FileWatcher { pub fn notify_hook_event(&self, path: std::path::PathBuf, kind: FileEventKind) -> Result<(), WatcherError> }`. Sends `FileEvent { path, kind, source: EventSource::PostToolUseHook }` via `self.sender.try_send(...)`; maps `TrySendError::Closed` → `WatcherError::ChannelClosed`, and `TrySendError::Full` → `WatcherError::ChannelClosed` (the channel is unbounded enough for typical hook rates; falling back to Closed on Full is acceptable for F02 — a follow-up WO can introduce backpressure). `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(self), fields(path = %path.display()))]` (span name `ucil.daemon.watcher.hook`).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — `fn map_notify_kind(kind: &notify::EventKind) -> FileEventKind`. Pure helper, module-private, pattern-matches on `notify::EventKind::{Create, Modify, Remove, Other, Access}`. `Access` maps to `FileEventKind::Other` (we don't care about reads for Phase-1 indexing). Used only by the forwarder task.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — Module-root tests (flat `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` or `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` at the top level — NO `mod tests { }` wrapper, per DEC-0005 and the WO-0023 `call_hierarchy::test_*` precedent). Use `tempfile::TempDir::new()?` and real `std::fs::write` for the notify-path tests. Use `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), rx.recv())` to bound every await on the receiver side (master-plan rust-style rule: every IO .await wrapped in timeout). Required tests (exact names):

* `test_notify_emits_event_after_debounce` — create watcher, write one file under the tempdir, assert one FileEvent arrives within 2s with `source == EventSource::NotifyDebounced` and `path` matching the written path. Debounce tolerance: accept any FileEventKind (notify's Create/Modify semantics vary by platform).

* `test_notify_debounces_editor_writes` — create watcher, write to the same file 5 times rapidly (sleep 5ms between writes, i.e., well under 100ms), then wait 2s; assert at most a small bounded number of events arrive (<= 3 — real platforms may split the 5 writes into 1-3 debounced batches). Primary assertion: the RAW event count on a debouncer-free path would be 5+; the debounced count is << 5. Exact bound of 3 is chosen to tolerate FSEvents/inotify variance.

* `test_post_tool_use_hook_bypasses_debounce` — create watcher, call `notify_hook_event(path, FileEventKind::Modified)` 3 times with no sleep between calls, assert all 3 events arrive within 100ms (the debounce window — proving the hook path DID bypass it) and each carries `source == EventSource::PostToolUseHook`.

* `test_hook_event_source_is_distinct` — assert that a hook-sourced event sets `source == EventSource::PostToolUseHook` while a notify-sourced event sets `source == EventSource::NotifyDebounced` (can be one composite test or folded into the two above; keep as a separate test to get a distinct frozen selector).

* `test_watcher_shutdown_is_clean` — create watcher, drop it (or shut down explicitly), then write a file; assert that the receiver channel either returns `None` from `recv()` OR that no new event is received within 500ms (both are valid semantics — a closed sender side means `None`, and an unresponsive watcher is detectable via timeout). The test passes if ONE of those two properties holds.

* `test_event_kind_mapping_covers_create_modify_remove` — unit-test `map_notify_kind` (since it's module-private, use a `#[cfg(test)]` `pub(crate)` re-exposure pattern OR call it inside the same test module — the flat-tests organisation makes that trivial). Assert `Create(_) -> Created`, `Modify(_) -> Modified`, `Remove(_) -> Removed`, `Access(_) -> Other`. Covers the F02 requirement that editor events are classified.

Every new public item carries a rustdoc block; cite master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741, §14 lines 1024-1025, and §15.2 (tracing spans). Every `.await` on IO wraps in `tokio::time::timeout` (rust-style rule). No `unwrap()`/`expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.

No new ADR unless the executor encounters a genuine spec ambiguity or a build failure with `notify` 6 on this toolchain. Version pinning (`notify = "6"`) is explicit to avoid a dep-resolution surprise.
scope_out: Watchman integration — that's P1-W3-F03 (separate WO). This WO MUST NOT reference `watchman_client` or add auto-detect logic.
PollWatcher fallback for network mounts — that's downstream of the Watchman WO (master-plan §3 line 138). Out of scope for F02.

Unix-socket / HTTP hook receiver — `notify_hook_event` is a sync function on `FileWatcher`; the socket plumbing (CLI hook → daemon) is a separate integration concern (likely a later daemon WO). F02's contract is the in-process API only.
Wiring the watcher into the parse/index pipeline — that's P1-W4-F04 (extraction pipeline). F02 stops at emitting typed `FileEvent`s into an mpsc channel; consumers come later.
Timestamp fields on `FileEvent` — hot-observation timestamps are a §12.1 concern, not a watcher concern. Ordering through the mpsc IS the only ordering contract F02 commits to.
Persistent subscriptions, cookie-based sync — those are Watchman-specific (F03).

Any `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper — DEC-0005 requires module-root flat tests. The `knowledge_graph::test_*` + `quality_pipeline::test_*` + `call_hierarchy::test_*` precedents (WO-0011/0016/0023) are the exemplars.
Mocks of `notify` or `notify-debouncer-full` — tests use real tempdir + real file writes (phase-log invariant 1).
Any change that removes the existing `lifecycle`, `plugin_manager`, `server`, `session_manager`, `session_ttl`, `storage` modules or their re-exports.

Any adjustment to clippy lint regime in `ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (keep `#![deny(warnings)]` + `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]` + `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` byte-for-byte).
acceptance: `pub mod watcher;` + re-export block `pub use watcher::{EventSource, FileEvent, FileEventKind, FileWatcher, WatcherError, DEBOUNCE_WINDOW};` appear in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`.

`crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs` exists with the four types (`FileEventKind`, `EventSource`, `FileEvent`, `WatcherError`) and `FileWatcher` struct + `new` + `notify_hook_event` methods + `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW` const + `map_notify_kind` helper.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon watcher::` green, matches ≥ 6 tests (the six listed in scope_in), all passing.
`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean (pedantic + nursery — same regime WO-0021 established).
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` clean (no `^warning` / `^error` lines; no broken intra-doc links — `lib.rs` already has `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`).
Module-root flat tests — no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `watcher.rs` — per DEC-0005.
`notify` + `notify-debouncer-full` entries present in both workspace `Cargo.toml` and `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`.
No edits outside the allowed paths (forbidden-paths guard).

Reality-check: `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W3-F02` passes (stashing `watcher.rs` causes the selector to miss OR the build to fail; popping restores). If the script trips on brand-new-module heuristics (WO-0014/0015/0016/0023 precedent; escalation `20260415-1630-reality-check-per-file-rollback.md`), the verifier performs the manual two-step instead — document the fallback in the ready-for-review marker.
No mocks of `notify` / `notify-debouncer-full` — tests use real `tempfile::TempDir` + `std::fs::write`.
Every `.await` on IO is wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout` (rust-style rule).
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -cE '^\s+PASS.*watcher::' | awk '{exit ($1 < 6)}'

cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon watcher::test_notify_emits_event_after_debounce --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -cE '^\s+PASS.*test_notify_emits_event_after_debounce' | awk '{exit ($1 != 1)}'

cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon watcher::test_post_tool_use_hook_bypasses_debounce --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -cE '^\s+PASS.*test_post_tool_use_hook_bypasses_debounce' | awk '{exit ($1 != 1)}'
context_refs: master-plan:§3 line 138 (file watcher: notify + notify-debouncer-full; Watchman optional upgrade — F03 scope; PollWatcher fallback scope also later)
master-plan:§14 lines 1024-1025 (two detection paths — agent-edits via PostToolUse hook bypass; human-edits via notify + 100ms debouncer)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741 (Week 3 file-watcher feature spec — the authoritative source for F02)

decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (module-root flat tests; frozen selector `watcher::` and `watcher::test_notify_emits_event_after_debounce` / `watcher::test_post_tool_use_hook_bypasses_debounce` are module-root paths)
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate (no cargo-mutants per WO; reality-check.sh is the mutation oracle)

work-order:WO-0021-daemon-lifecycle-and-session-state (recent success — `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs` is the precedent for adding a new module under ucil-daemon; mirrors its lib.rs re-export shape)

work-order:WO-0023-lsp-call-and-type-hierarchy-feed (recent success — flat module-root tests, 7 tests, PASSED on first verifier cycle; the `call_hierarchy::test_*` selector pattern is the F02 analogue for `watcher::test_*`)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (tokio::time::timeout on every IO await; thiserror; no unwrap outside cfg(test); pedantic + nursery clippy)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (Conventional Commits with Phase/Feature/Work-order trailers; push after each commit; no --amend after push; no force push)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariants 1 — no mocks of critical deps; 7 — every tokio::spawn's IO .await uses timeout)
harness:ucil-build/CLAUDE.md (planner writes to work-orders/; no flips from planner; verifier in fresh session is sole authority on passes flip)
lessons_applied: Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 — the frozen selector `watcher::` is a module-root path prefix. Mirrors WO-0011 `knowledge_graph::test_*`, WO-0016 `quality_pipeline::test_*`, WO-0023 `call_hierarchy::test_*` (all passed first verifier cycle).

No mocks of `notify` / `notify-debouncer-full` (phase-log invariant 1) — tests use real `tempfile::TempDir` + `std::fs::write`. The `tempfile` dev-dep is already wired into `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml:34`.

Every `.await` on IO wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout` (rust-style rule + phase-log invariant 7) — on the receiver side, always `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), rx.recv())` to bound test wall-time and surface deadlocks fast.

`notify-debouncer-full` produces events on its OWN thread (per notify's architecture). Bridge to async-land via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` + a forwarder that pushes into the tokio mpsc. Do NOT block the tokio runtime from the debouncer callback.

`FileEventKind` MUST be total — include `Renamed` (maps notify's `Rename` / `Modify::Name`) and `Other` (catch-all for metadata/access events we don't care about). Dropping unknown events silently is a future-debugging trap.

Debounce test tolerance — real inotify/FSEvents may split a run of rapid writes into 1-3 batches depending on kernel + filesystem. The `test_notify_debounces_editor_writes` assertion is `received_count <= 3` from 5 writes (not `== 1`). This avoids a platform-flake without sacrificing the debounce-vs-no-debounce delta signal.

`notify_hook_event` uses `sender.try_send(...)` (sync, non-blocking) so it's callable from a sync context AND cannot starve the async runtime. `TrySendError::Full` and `TrySendError::Closed` both map to `WatcherError::ChannelClosed` in this WO — backpressure semantics are a follow-up concern and out of scope for F02.

Rustdoc on every new public item cites master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1741 + §14 lines 1024-1025 (for `FileWatcher`, `EventSource` variants, `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW`). `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` must exit clean — `lib.rs:27` already has `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`, so any rustdoc error is a build error.

Clippy pedantic + nursery are in effect (`lib.rs:26`). Expect `missing_errors_doc`, `missing_const_for_fn`, `must_use_candidate`, `module_name_repetitions` on the additions. Fix, don't blanket-allow. `#[allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` at module top is permissible ONLY if fixing would force a non-idiomatic rename — document the `#[allow]` with a comment if used.

Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W3-F02`, `Work-order: WO-0026` trailers on every commit; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push. Target 6 commits, ~450 lines total (dep-wiring + types + watcher + hook + shutdown + mapping-helper).

Anti-abandonment safeguard (WO-0012/0020 was abandoned; WO-0024 was nearly abandoned) — before ending ANY session on this WO, the executor MUST verify: (a) `git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline` shows ≥ 4 commits on the feature branch, (b) `git diff origin/main..HEAD -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs | wc -l` returns > 150, (c) `git status --porcelain` is clean, (d) `git rev-parse HEAD` == `git rev-parse @{upstream}` (branch pushed), AND (e) `ucil-build/work-orders/0026-ready-for-review.md` exists. If any check fails, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the specific blocker.

Reality-check multi-commit false-positive — `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W3-F02` may trip the documented multi-commit rollback heuristic (escalation `20260415-1630-reality-check-per-file-rollback.md`). If so, the verifier performs the manual two-step mutation instead (rolling back `watcher.rs` + the `lib.rs` re-export line to `origin/main` content, asserting `watcher::` nextest selector fails or returns 0 tests; restoring; asserting ≥ 6 tests pass). The executor should document this fallback expectation in `0026-ready-for-review.md` so the verifier doesn't re-flag.

`notify` 6.x + `notify-debouncer-full` 0.3.x is a compatible pair (both maintained by the notify-rs org). If the dep resolver surfaces an incompatibility on this toolchain, raise an ADR `DEC-NNNN-notify-version-alignment.md` rather than silently choosing a different major.

commit_plan.subject: add notify + notify-debouncer-full workspace deps + wire into ucil-daemon
watcher: add FileEvent + FileEventKind + EventSource + WatcherError + DEBOUNCE_WINDOW; module declaration + lib.rs re-exports
watcher: FileWatcher::new wires notify-debouncer-full to tokio mpsc; forwarder task; test_notify_emits_event_after_debounce + test_notify_debounces_editor_writes
watcher: notify_hook_event bypass-debounce path; test_post_tool_use_hook_bypasses_debounce + test_hook_event_source_is_distinct
watcher: shutdown semantics via Drop; test_watcher_shutdown_is_clean + final rustdoc polish
